zh.lifeSummary: 當Anna Margaretha Kim於1667年1月9日出生在德國海藍-普法爾茲時，她的父親Maximilian Kihm30歲，母親Gertrud HENSEL19歲。她於1691年1月9日與Johann Adam Wagner在德國貝也普法爾茲結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1741年8月1日在德國貝也普法爾茲享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anna Margaretha Kim于1667年1月9日出生在德国海藍-普法爾茲时，她的父亲Maximilian Kihm30岁，母亲Gertrud HENSEL19岁。她于1691年1月9日与Johann Adam Wagner在德国貝也普法爾茲结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1741年8月1日在德国貝也普法爾茲享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Kim
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Yong, Sung, Chong, Jung, Sang, Jong, Jin, Chang, Kyung, Dong, Kwang, Jae, Myung, Byung, Chung, Dae, Chul, Myong, Moon, Nam, Seong, Jeong, Min.

Korean (김): there is one Chinese character for the surname Kim (金, meaning ‘gold’). Kim is the most common Korean surname, comprising about 20 percent of the Korean population. According to some sources, there are over 600 different Kim clans, but only c. 100 have been documented. Kims can be found in virtually every part of Korea. The two largest Kim clans, the Kim family of Kimhae and the Kim family of Kyŏngju, are descended from semi-mythological characters who lived two thousand years ago. According to legend, the Kimhae Kim family founder, Kim Suro, came in answer to a prayer offered by the nine elders of the ancient Karak Kingdom. In 42 AD , these elders met together to pray for a king. In answer to their prayer, they were sent a golden box containing six golden eggs. From the first egg emerged King Su-ro, Karak's first king. The other five eggs became the five kings of Karak's neighboring kingdom, Kaya. The founder of the Kim family of Kyŏngju, Kim Al-ji, had similar origins. In 65 AD the king of Shilla, T’alhae, heard a strange sound from a forest near the Shilla capital, Kyŏngju. On investigation he found a crowing white rooster standing next to a golden egg. From this egg emerged Al-ji, founder of the Kyŏngju Kim family and subsequent king of the Shilla Kingdom. Because Al-ji emerged from a golden egg, King T’alhae bestowed upon the child the surname Kim, which means ‘gold’. It is estimated that about half of the one hundred or so Kim clans of modern Korea are descended from the Kyŏngju Kim clan.

Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, and Taiwanese form of the surname 金, meaning ‘gold’, see   Jin   1. This surname is found mainly in other parts of Southeast Asia, to which Chinese people migrated from Taiwan, Fujian province, and eastern Guangdong province.

variants.name: Kon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Hideo, Masayuki, Akio, Atsushi, Hideaki, Hiroshi, Kinuko, Kumiko, Masao, Megumi, Sadao, Shigenori.  Jewish Basya, Dov, Henia, Isak, Zalman.

Japanese: the most usual rendering is 金 ‘gold’; other characters, apparently used phonetically, include 今 and 近. It is rare in Japan; bearers are most likely descended from immigrants named   Kim  , from the ancient Korean Shilla kingdom.

variants.name: Jin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Wei, Yi, Sung, Yan, Hong, Jian, Jin, Li, Dong, Lei, Peng, Byung, Chang, Chang Hyun, Chul, Dae, Hak, Jeong, Seong, Tian, Yiping, Young Woo, Byung Soon.  Japanese Yu, Kaoru, Yue, Akiko, Chiaki, Hiroshi, Kyo, Naohiro, Susumu, Takashi, Yumi.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 金, meaning ‘gold’ in Chinese: (i) from Jin Tian (金天), title of the legendary king Shao Hao (c. 26th century BC ), the son of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). Some of his descendants inherited 金, part of his title, as their surname. (ii) from Jin Midi (金日磾) (134–86 BC ), a prince of King Xiu Tu of the Huns, who was captured as a child and brought up at the Han imperial court. He became an official highly regarded by Emperor Wu of Han (156–87 BC ) and was appointed as master of the imperial stables. He made golden statues of horses to worship Heaven and because of this was given the surname Jin, 金 (meaning ‘gold’) by the emperor. (iii) for some families, the surname is traced back to the Liu (劉, see Lau) family living in the state of Wu Yue (907–978 AD , located in present-day Zhejiang, Jiangsu, and Fujian provinces), who changed their original surname 劉 to 金, because the former was pronounced the same as the personal name of the emperor Qian Liu (founder of the Wuyue Kingdom, 852–932 AD ), and it was taboo in ancient China to bear a name that was borne by the emperor. (iv) for some families, the surname is traced back to the minority ethnic groups of Qiangzu and Xinluo in northern China during the Southern and Northern dynasties (420–589 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 靳, meaning ‘stingy’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Jin (靳), the name of a fief (located in Ningxiang in Hunan province) granted to Jin Shang (靳尚) (died 311  BC ), a noble official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) for some families, the surname can be traced back to the Huns in northern China, recorded in ancient Chinese history books.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

nameMeanings.name: Margaretha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaret
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margrethe
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

en.lifeSummary: When Anna Margaretha Kim was born on 9 January 1667, in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, her father, Maximilian Kihm, was 30 and her mother, Gertrud HENSEL, was 19. She married Johann Adam Wagner on 9 January 1691, in Palatinate, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 1 August 1741, in Palatinate, Bavaria, Germany, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anna Margaretha Kim est née le 9 janvier 1667, à Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, son père, Maximilian Kihm, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Gertrud HENSEL, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Johann Adam Wagner le 9 janvier 1691, à Pfalz, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1741, à Pfalz, Bayern, Allemagne, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anna Margaretha Kim am 9. Januar 1667 in Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Maximilian Kihm 30 und ihre Mutter Gertrud HENSEL 19. Sie hat am 9. August 1741 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Pfalz, Bayern, Deutschland.

Lei sposato Johann Adam Wagner il 9 gennaio 1691, nel Pfalz, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 1 agosto 1741, nel Pfalz, Bayern, Germania, all'età di 74 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Margaretha Kim(이)가 1667년 1월 9일 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Maximilian Kihm(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gertrud HENSEL(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1691년 1월 9일 독일 베이른 p팔즈에서 Johann Adam Wagner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1741년 8월 1일 독일 베이른 p팔즈에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Johann Adam Wagner em 9 de janeiro de 1691, em Pfalz, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anna Margaretha Kim родилась 9 января 1667 г. в местности раинланд-пфалз, Германия, её отцу по имени Maximilian Kihm было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertrud HENSEL, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Adam Wagner 9 января 1691 г. в местности пфалз, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 1 августа 1741 г. в местности пфалз, беиирн, Германия в возрасте 74 лет.

Se casó con Johann Adam Wagner el 9 de enero de 1691, en Pfalz, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1741, en Pfalz, Bayern, Alemania, a la edad de 74 años.
